$comment: Phases for the dirgha-cli Hermes-parity rebuild. Each phase has: name (id), goal, must_read (files the agent should consult), must_not_touch (paths off-limits), acceptance (shell command — exit 0 = pass), max_iters.

phases.name: phase-0-baseline
phases.goal:
Write docs/HERMES_PARITY_BASELINE.md (relative to repo root) listing exactly five known-broken behaviors of the v1.3.0 binary in a markdown checklist:
  1. /help shows '0 commands' (slash registry not plumbed into Ink TUI)
  2. Model picker intercepts keystrokes after close
  3. Streaming events flow in NDJSON mode but render silently in interactive Ink TUI
  4. No per-model context_window enforcement; compaction code at src_v2/context/compaction.ts is dead
  5. Model registry is split across prices.ts, dispatch.ts, openrouter.ts, setup.ts (single source of truth missing)
For each item include a one-line root cause hypothesis and the file:line where it lives. Then call the `done` tool. Do NOT modify any source file.
phases.acceptance: test -f docs/HERMES_PARITY_BASELINE.md && grep -qc 'slash' docs/HERMES_PARITY_BASELINE.md && grep -qc 'picker' docs/HERMES_PARITY_BASELINE.md && grep -qc 'context_window' docs/HERMES_PARITY_BASELINE.md

phases.name: phase-1a-slash-plumbing
phases.goal:
Plumb the built-in slash command registry into the Ink TUI so /help shows all 20 commands instead of 0.

Problem location: src_v2/cli/main.ts at the runInkTUI({...}) call — currently doesn't pass slashCommands.

Changes required:
  1. Import builtinSlashCommands from './slash/index.js' in src_v2/cli/main.ts
  2. Map each command to { name, description, aliases? } and pass as `slashCommands` field to runInkTUI
  3. Run `npm run build:v2` from repo root and verify it succeeds

Do NOT touch any other file. Do NOT change tests. After build succeeds, call done.
phases.acceptance: npm run build:v2 2>&1 | tail -5 && grep -q 'builtinSlashCommands' dist_v2/cli/main.js && grep -q 'slashCommands' dist_v2/cli/main.js

phases.name: phase-1b-picker-isactive
phases.goal:
Fix the ModelPicker so its useInput hook stops handling keystrokes when the picker is closed.

File: src_v2/tui/ink/components/ModelPicker.tsx
The useInput call near the top of the component currently runs unconditionally. Add a second arg `{ isActive: true }` won't help because the picker is mounted only when shown — but verify that and confirm. The actual bug may be elsewhere: investigate whether ModelPicker is mounted while overlay is closed (check App.tsx render block).

Deliverable: either (a) confirm the picker is correctly mounted only when overlay open and document this in a comment in App.tsx near the render, OR (b) add an explicit `isActive` guard if you find a real leak.

Run `npm run build:v2` to verify TypeScript still compiles. Then call done.
phases.acceptance: npm run build:v2 2>&1 | tail -3 && test -f dist_v2/tui/ink/components/ModelPicker.js
